title: Michael Jackson Official Website
text: Michael Jackson was one of the most influential artists in popular music. His official website presents information about his albums, videos, songs, performances, and legacy.

title: Michael Jackson Biography
text: Michael Jackson was born on August 29, 1958, in Gary, Indiana. He became an American singer, songwriter, dancer, and entertainer with a major impact on popular music.

title: Childhood in Gary Indiana
text: Michael Jackson grew up in Gary, Indiana, in a large musical family. His childhood was closely connected to singing, rehearsals, and performance with his brothers.

title: Family Background
text: Michael Jackson was part of the Jackson family, one of the most famous musical families in popular music. His family background strongly influenced his early career.

title: Joseph Jackson Father
text: Joseph Jackson was Michael Jackson's father and an important figure in the early career of the Jackson family. He helped organize and manage the musical development of his children.

title: Katherine Jackson Mother
text: Katherine Jackson was Michael Jackson's mother. She was part of the family environment that supported the children's musical development and early performance life.

title: Early Musical Training
text: Michael Jackson started performing at a very young age. Early rehearsals and performances helped him develop his voice, rhythm, confidence, and stage presence.

title: Jackson 5 Formation
text: The Jackson 5 was a family music group that included Michael Jackson and his brothers. Michael became the lead singer while he was still a child.

title: Jackson 5 and Motown
text: The Jackson 5 signed with Motown Records in 1969. The group became famous with energetic performances, catchy songs, and Michael Jackson's strong lead vocals.

title: I Want You Back
text: I Want You Back was one of the famous early hits of the Jackson 5. The song helped introduce Michael Jackson's young voice and performance energy to a wide audience.

title: ABC
text: ABC was one of the best-known songs by the Jackson 5. It showed the group's youthful pop and soul sound and became part of Michael Jackson's early musical identity.

title: I'll Be There
text: I'll Be There was an important Jackson 5 song that highlighted emotional singing and helped show Michael Jackson's vocal ability at a young age.

title: Michael as Lead Singer
text: Michael Jackson became the lead singer of the Jackson 5 as a child. His voice, rhythm, and stage confidence made him stand out within the group.

title: From Jackson 5 to Solo Career
text: Michael Jackson first became famous as a member of the Jackson 5 and later became a global solo artist. This transition was one of the most important parts of his career.

title: The Jacksons Era
text: After the Jackson 5 period, the group continued as The Jacksons. This era allowed the brothers to develop more control over their music and performances.

title: Off the Wall Album
text: Off the Wall was released in 1979 and became an important album in Michael Jackson's solo career. It connected pop, disco, soul, and dance music.

title: Don't Stop Til You Get Enough
text: Don't Stop Til You Get Enough is one of the major songs from the Off the Wall era. It showed Michael Jackson's energetic solo sound and dance-oriented style.

title: Rock With You
text: Rock With You is a well-known song from the Off the Wall period. It reflects Michael Jackson's smooth vocal style and connection to disco and pop music.

title: Thriller Album
text: Thriller was released in 1982 and became one of Michael Jackson's most famous albums. It includes songs such as Thriller, Billie Jean, Beat It, and Human Nature.

title: Billie Jean
text: Billie Jean is one of Michael Jackson's most iconic songs. It is known for its bassline, vocal performance, visual style, and connection to his moonwalk performance.

title: Beat It
text: Beat It is one of the major songs from Thriller. It helped connect pop, rock, and dance elements and became one of Michael Jackson's recognizable tracks.

title: Thriller Song and Video
text: Thriller became famous as both a song and a music video. The video used cinematic storytelling, choreography, and theatrical style.

title: Motown 25 and Moonwalk
text: Michael Jackson's moonwalk became one of his most famous performance symbols. It is closely connected to his Billie Jean performances and his image as a unique dancer.

title: Moonwalk
text: The moonwalk is one of Michael Jackson's most famous dance moves. It became a signature part of his performance style and global image.

title: Bad Album
text: Bad was released in 1987 and continued Michael Jackson's global success after Thriller. The album strengthened his image as a solo pop performer.

title: Bad Song and Image
text: Bad is connected to Michael Jackson's stronger visual image in the late 1980s. The song and video showed confidence, choreography, and a more dramatic performance style.

title: The Way You Make Me Feel
text: The Way You Make Me Feel is one of Michael Jackson's well-known songs from the Bad era. It shows his energetic pop sound and confident performance style.

title: Man in the Mirror
text: Man in the Mirror is one of Michael Jackson's important songs with a message about personal change, reflection, and social responsibility.

title: Smooth Criminal
text: Smooth Criminal is known for its dramatic rhythm, sharp choreography, and stylish visual performance. It became one of Michael Jackson's signature songs.

title: Dangerous Album
text: Dangerous was released in 1991 and showed a new sound in Michael Jackson's career. It included pop, R&B, dance, and new jack swing influences.

title: Black or White
text: Black or White became one of Michael Jackson's famous songs from the Dangerous era. It is connected to themes of unity, identity, and global pop culture.

title: Remember the Time
text: Remember the Time is a well-known song from the Dangerous period. It is remembered for its rhythm, visual style, and connection to Michael Jackson's 1990s sound.

title: Heal the World
text: Heal the World is one of Michael Jackson's songs with a humanitarian message. It reflects themes of peace, care, and social responsibility.

title: HIStory Album
text: HIStory: Past, Present and Future, Book I was released in 1995. It combined earlier hits with newer material and represented both reflection and new artistic direction.

title: They Don't Care About Us
text: They Don't Care About Us is one of Michael Jackson's socially focused songs. It is connected to themes of injustice, protest, and public awareness.

title: Scream
text: Scream is a song from the HIStory era. It reflects pressure, media attention, and frustration through a strong visual and musical style.

title: You Are Not Alone
text: You Are Not Alone is one of Michael Jackson's well-known songs from the HIStory period. It presents a softer emotional side of his music.

title: Blood on the Dance Floor
text: Blood on the Dance Floor: HIStory in the Mix was released in 1997. It connected remix material with new songs and dance-oriented production.

title: Invincible Album
text: Invincible was released in 2001 and was one of Michael Jackson's later studio albums. It showed his continued interest in contemporary pop and R&B production.

title: Music Videos
text: Michael Jackson helped change the role of music videos in popular music. His videos used storytelling, choreography, fashion, and cinematic presentation.

title: Dance Style
text: Michael Jackson influenced dance through sharp choreography, body control, rhythm, and memorable stage movements. His dancing became a central part of his global image.

title: Stage Performance
text: Michael Jackson's stage performances combined music, dance, costume, lighting, and theatrical presentation. His concerts helped shape the expectations of large pop shows.

title: Bad World Tour
text: The Bad World Tour was Michael Jackson's first major solo world tour. It supported the Bad album and showed his ability to perform as a global concert artist.

title: Dangerous World Tour
text: The Dangerous World Tour was connected to the Dangerous album era. It included large concerts, choreography, dramatic staging, and international performances.

title: HIStory World Tour
text: The HIStory World Tour was connected to the HIStory era. It showed Michael Jackson's continued global popularity and large-scale performance style.

title: This Is It Concerts
text: This Is It was Michael Jackson's planned concert project near the end of his life. A film featuring rehearsals for the concerts was released after his death.

title: GRAMMY Awards
text: Michael Jackson received major recognition from the GRAMMY Awards. His GRAMMY profile highlights important wins, nominations, and major career moments.

title: Eight GRAMMYs in One Night
text: At the 1984 GRAMMYs, Michael Jackson became the first artist to win eight GRAMMY Awards in one night. This became one of the most famous award moments of his career.

title: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
text: Michael Jackson was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame as a member of the Jackson 5 and later as a solo artist. This reflects his major influence on popular music.

title: Death and Legacy
text: Michael Jackson died on June 25, 2009, in Los Angeles, California, at age 50. His legacy continued through his music, videos, dance style, and influence on later performers.
